data.title: Meta launches stablecoin payments for creators; Federal Reserve keeps interest rates unchanged.
data.description:
Headline

▌Meta Launches Stablecoin Payment Function for Creators via Stripe

On April 30th, Meta partnered with Stripe to launch a stablecoin payment function for creators, supporting Circle's USDC. Based on the Solana and Polygon blockchain networks, the initial implementation will begin with creators in Colombia and the Philippines.

▌Federal Reserve Keeps Benchmark Interest Rate Target Range at 3.5%-3.75%, in Line with Market Expectations

On April 30th, the Federal Reserve kept its benchmark interest rate target range unchanged at 3.5%-3.75%, in line with market expectations. This marks the third consecutive year that the Fed has announced it will maintain the current interest rate level, which has been maintained since the last rate cut in December 2025.

The interest rate was kept unchanged at the policy meetings in January, March and April 2026, which was in line with the market's expectation of "holding steady". As of press time, according to CoinGecko data: BTC price is $75,785.94, a 24-hour change of -0.6%; ETH price is $2,254.91, a 24-hour change of -1.4%; BNB price is $617.75, a 24-hour change of -1.0%. SOL price is $82.94, a 24-hour change of -1.1%; DOGE price is $0.1038, a 24-hour change of +4.6%; XRP price is $1.37, a 24-hour change of -0.9%; TRX price is $0.323, a 24-hour change of +0.1%. WLFI price is $0.06335, a 24-hour change of -14.1%; HYPE price is $39.91, a 24-hour change of -0.4%. Policy: Senator Tillis: Ready to Push Clarity Act into Committee Review. On April 29th, Fox Business reporter Eleanor Terrett revealed that Republican Senator Thom Tillis stated he is prepared to push the Clarity Act into the committee review stage. "We've made a lot of progress, and it's time to put the bill on the committee and move it forward," Tillis said in an interview on Capitol Hill. He stated that he would request the committee chairman to schedule a formal review once lawmakers reconvene. Regarding the controversy surrounding stablecoin yields, Tillis said that most of the banking industry's concerns have been heard and addressed, and welcomed continued "goodwill participation in the discussion." On the timeline, Tillis said he hopes to release the legislative text regarding stablecoin yield provisions four to five days before formal review and provide a preview version to relevant stakeholders in advance. Furthermore, regarding software developer liability and the potential impact of the 1960 Criminal Code on law enforcement, Tillis expressed overall support for the legislative direction currently being pushed forward by Cynthia Lummis.

▌Korea's National Tax Service Initiates Preparations for Virtual Asset Taxation, Planned for January 2027

On April 29, the Korea National Tax Service initiated preparations for virtual asset taxation, aiming for formal implementation in January 2027 and preparing for the May 2028 comprehensive income tax filing. Under the current income tax law, income from the transfer and leasing of virtual assets will be classified as "other income," with a 22% tax rate levied on annual income exceeding 2.5 million won, expected to affect approximately 13.26 million people. The Korea National Tax Service plans to obtain data from trading platforms such as Upbit, Bithumb, and Coinone starting next year to improve the taxation infrastructure and promote the launch of a comprehensive virtual asset analysis system within the year. However, controversies surrounding tax standards and the risk of capital outflow continue.

...

▌Putin: Prepared for Ceasefire During Victory Day

On April 30, according to a report from Russian Presidential Aide Ushakov on the evening of the 29th, Russian President Putin stated during a phone call with US President Trump that Russia is prepared to implement a ceasefire during Victory Day, and Trump expressed his support. (Xinhua News Agency)

Blockchain Applications

▌Amazon Launches OpenClaw-like Desktop AI Agent and Releases Several Independent AI Agent Services

On April 29, Amazon launched a desktop AI agent similar to OpenClaw and simultaneously released several independent AI agent services. This desktop AI agent is a new version of the existing cloud service Amazon Quick. Amazon Quick can perform various tasks for users, such as drafting emails and extracting information from personal data.

... Amazon states that Quick was designed to enable cross-ecosystem collaboration, supporting platforms including Microsoft 365, Google Workspace, Slack, and Zoom. The core concept is that Quick can access and utilize data regardless of its location. Amazon emphasizes that Quick has built-in security mechanisms, proactively requesting permission before accessing data or performing tasks for users. Amazon also announced the expansion of its AI-powered customer support platform, Amazon Connect, into four distinct agent-based AI services: Connect Decisions for supply chain management, Connect Talent for recruitment, Connect Customer AI for customer support, and Connect Health, designed specifically for the healthcare system. (Dongxin News Agency) MoonPay announced the acquisition of Israeli crypto security startup Sodot in a $100 million all-stock transaction. Following the acquisition, Sodot will be integrated into MoonPay Institutional, a newly established division. MoonPay Institutional will reportedly provide various cryptocurrency and blockchain services to large traditional financial institutions, including trading, tokenized securities, payments, wallet management, and stablecoin issuance. Parallel Web Systems, the AI ​​infrastructure company founded by former Twitter CEO Parag Agrawal, completed a $100 million Series B funding round on April 29th, according to the Wall Street Journal. This brings its post-money valuation to $2 billion. This round was led by Sequoia Capital, with participation from existing investors including Kleiner Perkins, Index Ventures, and Khosla Ventures. Parallel provides efficient web search and access control infrastructure for "long-cycle" AI agents. It has raised a total of $230 million, has approximately 50 employees, and serves over 100,000 developers and enterprise clients. Currently, its focus is on expanding its sales, marketing, and R&D teams, and continuing to develop enterprise-level "AI agent network usage" scenarios.

▌Squads Completes $18 Million Strategic Funding Round Led by Solana Ventures

Squads announced the completion of an $18 million strategic funding round, led by Solana Ventures, with participation from Coinbase Ventures, Haun Ventures, L1D, and others, bringing its total funding to $42.9 million. This round of financing was completed last December but was only recently made public.

... Squads' stablecoin payment platform, Altitude, allows businesses to conduct 24/7 global payment settlements in stablecoin form through self-custodied wallets and access global payment networks through compliance and risk control systems.

▌Blockchain finance company Fence completes $20 million funding round, led by Galaxy Digital

Blockchain finance company Fence announced the completion of a new $20 million funding round, led by Galaxy Digital, with participation from Parafi Capital and Crane Venture Partners. Fence focuses on using blockchain and tokenization technologies to transform the back-office processes of the asset-backed finance market, primarily targeting the operational layer of structured credit transactions, including loan pool tracking, collateral verification, and cash flow.

...

▌Belo Raises $14 Million in Series A Funding, Led by Tether

On April 29th, Latin American digital wallet Belo raised $14 million in Series A funding, led by stablecoin issuer Tether, with participation from Titan Fund, The Venture City, Mindset Ventures, and G2. The company plans to use this funding to expand to Mexico, Chile, Colombia, Peru, Bolivia, and Paraguay, and deepen its business presence in Brazil, focusing on serving freelancers and remote workers making cross-border payments, reducing cross-border payment and foreign exchange costs and friction through stablecoin infrastructure.

Cryptocurrency

▌Tether Wallet Announces BTC Tap Launch

On April 29th, Tether Wallet announced the official launch of its BTC tap.

▌Michael Saylor: The goal is to push Bitcoin to $10 million per coin

On April 29th, Strategy founder Michael Saylor stated in his speech at the Bitcoin 2026 conference that the goal is to push the price of Bitcoin to $10 million per coin and make it a $200 trillion value network, while continuing to grow digital banking accounts for 1 billion people globally.

...

▌Vitalik Sells 40 Million ASTEROID-2 MEME Tokens for 114,566 USDC

On April 29th, according to Onchain Lens monitoring, Vitalik has begun selling off the MEME-like tokens he received as gifts. He sold 40 million $ASTEROID-2 tokens, cashing out 114,566 USDC. It is highly likely that he will continue to sell other gifted tokens in the future.

...

▌Vanguard Group's fund increases its holdings in Strive to 1.72 million shares, worth approximately $25.2 million

On April 29, according to BitcoinTreasuries.NET, Vanguard Group's Total Stock Market Index Fund, which manages $12 trillion in assets, increased its holdings in Bitcoin vault company Strive (ASST) by 276,200 shares, bringing its total holdings to 1.72 million shares, worth approximately $25.2 million.

▌Coinbase to Cease DAI Trading from May 4th

On April 30th, Coinbase announced that it will cease Dai (DAI) trading on the Coinbase website and Coinbase mobile app starting May 4th, 2026, and will suspend DAI deposits and withdrawals from May 4th to 6th. At that time, any remaining DAI on the platform will be automatically converted to USDS at a ratio of 1 DAI: 1 USDS. Users who do not wish to be automatically converted must transfer their DAI to a compatible self-custodied wallet before May 4th.

... Coinbase will launch perpetual contracts for AMD, ARM, Intel, and more on April 29, 2026. The AMD-PERP, ARM-PERP, INTC-PERP, MU-PERP, and SNDK-PERP markets will open after liquidity conditions are met. On April 30, Coinbase announced it would add support for MegaETH (MEGA), initially only in regions where trading is supported.

▌Coinbase to Launch Wrapped Ronin (WRON) Spot Trading

On April 30, Coinbase announced that it will launch Wrapped Ronin (WRON) spot trading, provided that liquidity conditions are met and trading is limited to supported regions.

Important Economic Developments

▌Trump Says He Will Continue Naval Blockade Against Iran

On April 30, according to a report by the US news website Axios on the 29th, US President Trump said in an interview that he will continue the naval blockade against Iran until Iran agrees to an agreement that can alleviate US concerns about Iran's nuclear program.

(Xinhua News Agency) US Treasury Secretary Bessant: Powell's decision to remain on the board would go against tradition. On April 30, US Treasury Secretary Bessant stated that it would be unusual for outgoing Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell to remain on the Federal Reserve Board. For someone who has always emphasized discipline, his unilateral decision goes against tradition. Kevin Warsh will bring a fresh perspective to the Fed with a clear system of accountability, effective management mechanisms, and sound policymaking. The Fed describes inflation as "still high" rather than "slightly high" in its previous statement. On April 30, the Federal Reserve described inflation as "still high," rather than "slightly high" in its previous statement, citing global energy prices. On average, job growth remains low. The Federal Open Market Committee (FOMC) seeks to achieve full employment and a 2% inflation rate in the long run. Developments in the Middle East are introducing significant uncertainty into the economic outlook. The Committee is highly concerned about the risks to its dual mandate (full employment and price stability).

▌Federal Reserve Chairman Powell: Committed to Restoring Inflation to 2%

On April 30, Federal Reserve Chairman Powell stated that the commitment to restoring inflation to 2% is strong, and striving to achieve the 2% inflation target could be costly. He hopes to gradually reduce inflation to 2% while minimizing damage as much as possible.

▌Fed Does Not Signal When to Adjust Interest Rates

On April 30, the Federal Reserve did not signal when to adjust interest rates, citing significant uncertainty from the Middle East situation.

▌The Federal Reserve says the policy vote was 8 to 4

On April 30, the Federal Reserve said the policy vote was 8 to 4: Milan voted against it, advocating a 25 basis point rate cut; Hamak, Kashkari, and Logan also voted against including a dovish bias in the statement. This is the largest number of dissenting votes in a Fed interest rate decision and policy statement since October 1992.

▌Market pricing indicates a greater likelihood of a Fed rate hike this year than a rate cut

On April 30, according to the Wall Street Journal, after some hawkish signals from Fed officials, Wall Street traders are betting that the Fed may raise interest rates this year, although the probability is still small.

CME interest rate futures data shows that traders believe there is an 11% chance of a Federal Reserve rate hike this year, up from 5% earlier in the day and 0% on Tuesday, while the probability of a rate cut hovers around 2%. The probability of the Fed keeping rates unchanged in June is 98.6%. According to CME's "FedWatch" on April 30th, the probability of the Fed keeping rates unchanged by June is 98.6% (98.8% before the decision), and the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut is 1.4% (1.2% before the decision). The probability of the Fed keeping rates unchanged by July is 96.5% (94.6% before the decision), and the probability of a cumulative 25 basis point rate cut is 3.4% (5.4% before the decision). The probability of the Federal Reserve maintaining interest rates unchanged by September is 96.1% (92.7% before the decision), and the probability of a cumulative rate cut of 25 basis points is 3.8% (7.2% before the decision). [Golden Encyclopedia] [Can Bitcoin Still Be Called "Digital Gold" During Geopolitical Crises?] Every time a geopolitical crisis occurs, gold prices rise while Bitcoin prices plummet. After six tests, the claim of "digital gold" has never been confirmed by data. Countries hoard gold but exclude Bitcoin from their reserves. For investors, Bitcoin exhibits asymmetry: it falls with stocks but does not rise with them. Three structural asymmetries prevent Bitcoin from achieving safe-haven status: an oversupply of derivatives (market structure), the dominance of leveraged traders (participant composition), and a lack of repeated behavioral records (behavioral accumulation). Bitcoin is not a safe-haven asset, but it is a "crisis-prone asset," capable of functioning effectively when borders are closed and banks fail. If these three asymmetries narrow, Bitcoin may no longer be a replica of gold, but rather a completely new "next-generation gold." Generational shifts and the widespread adoption of algorithms are key factors that could accelerate this process.

data.title: In-depth research on the Telegram guarantee market
data.description:
The Telegram escrow market has gradually formed an underground service system integrating escrow matching, fund settlement, merchant management, and traffic distribution, exhibiting clear platform-based and network-based development characteristics. Looking at the development paths of leading platforms, the succession of Huione, Tudou, and Xinbi demonstrates that the development of the Telegram escrow market is not simply dependent on the expansion of a single platform, but rather undergoes a dynamic evolution process of platform contraction, demand spillover, and new nodes taking over under continuous regulatory and enforcement pressure. Although several leading platforms have been impacted by regulation, enforcement, or sanctions, market demand has not disappeared simultaneously, but has continued to migrate to other platforms, merchant networks, and alternative tools. The use of new private domain tools, such as SafeW (a privacy communication tool similar to Telegram), and the frequent changes in platform addresses reflect that the ecosystem is shifting towards a more decentralized, covert, and resilient approach. Overall, while targeted attacks can suppress the activity of leading platforms, the continued governance of the entire Telegram escrow ecosystem still faces long-term challenges of cross-platform migration and rapid restructuring. I. Current Overview of Telegram Escrow Services Over the past few years, the "escrow service," which has developed based on the Telegram ecosystem, has gradually evolved from an auxiliary tool for solving trust issues in anonymous transactions into a crucial infrastructure supporting the underground economy. These services, by introducing third-party guarantors, use on-chain crypto assets (primarily USDT) for fund custody and transaction matching, building an alternative credit mechanism in an environment lacking legal constraints and identity trust. Unlike traditional regulated guarantee or payment systems, Telegram's escrow service inherently possesses cross-border, anonymity, and low-barrier-to-entry characteristics, enabling it to rapidly penetrate and expand in various high-risk and even illegal transaction scenarios. From its early transaction model primarily based on simple intermediary matching to its current complex structure integrating merchant systems, automated bots, and fund collection and distribution capabilities, its functional boundaries have significantly expanded, no longer limited to a "transaction security tool," but gradually acquiring multiple attributes of "trading market + clearing/settlement network + credit intermediary." In this evolution, the escrow platform not only carries a large number of high-frequency fund transfer activities but also objectively forms a key liquidity node in the underground economy. Especially in fund transfer businesses such as acceptance, collection and payment on behalf of others, and money laundering, the guarantee mechanism has become a crucial hub connecting the supply and demand sides of funds, enabling previously scattered illegal transactions to operate on a large scale and systematically. It is noteworthy that despite the continuous strengthening of supervision and enforcement against related platforms in recent years, market demand has not disappeared; instead, it has driven the continuous evolution of guarantee service models, moving from centralized platforms to decentralized, multi-node, and even tool-based approaches. This change signifies that the Telegram guarantee system is transforming from a "single target that can be identified and targeted" into a "networked structure with self-reconstruction capabilities." Based on this, this paper attempts to focus on the infrastructure role of the Telegram guarantee platform in the underground economy, combining publicly available intelligence and on-chain fund data to analyze its business structure, evolutionary path, fund characteristics, and impact on AML risk control. II. Business Scope Introduction The Telegram escrow platform is essentially a comprehensive black and gray market that integrates credit guarantee services. Its business can be categorized into four main types based on its function within the black and gray market chain: fund transfer, resource supply, technical services, and high-risk applications. These four categories together constitute a complete underground industry closed loop. Figure 1: Panoramic View of Guarantee Market Business 1. Fund Flow Category This type of business directly revolves around the flow of "money," and is the most core and largest part of the guarantee system in terms of fund volume. It is also a key area that needs attention in the AML field. Its main types include:

  • Acceptance business: Exchange between crypto assets (USDT) and fiat currency (USDT in, fiat currency out / fiat currency in, USDT out)

  • Collection and payment on behalf: Using third-party accounts to complete the collection and distribution of funds, or using "relatively clean funds" to provide payment outlets for upstream black funds

  • Money laundering/money mixing: Typical underground money laundering networks that provide cleansing services for numerous black and gray industry funds.

  • Money laundering/money mixing: Typical underground money laundering networks that provide cleansing services for numerous black and gray industry funds.

Core Characteristics:

  • Strong reliance on guarantee platforms as credit intermediaries

  • Funds exhibit high frequency, small-amount splitting and re-aggregation characteristics

  • Addresses exhibit high frequency changes and large transaction volumes

  • Widely used in the withdrawal process after fraud, gambling, and laundering of illicit funds

The essential function of this type of business is to complete the "transfer, replacement, and path restructuring" of illegal funds.

2. Resource Supply Type

This type of business provides "usable resources" for various illegal activities and belongs to the basic support layer of black and gray industries.

Highly coupled with fund transfer (used to receive funds)

The essential function is to provide a "landing carrier" and "identity shell" for fund flow.

3. Technology and Service Category

This type of business does not directly generate funds or resources, but significantly improves the "efficiency" and "scale capability" of black and gray industries.

Highly coupled with fund transfer (used to receive funds)

This type of business does not directly generate funds or resources, but significantly improves the "efficiency" and "scale capability" of black and gray industries.

Main types include: Fraud technical support: phishing websites, fake pages, Trojan programs; Traffic generation and customer acquisition: social media placement, scripted chat, targeted data packages; System setup: scoring systems, payment channel systems, backend management panels; Risk control and avoidance services: anti-detection strategies, IP proxies, device environment spoofing. Core features: Obvious trend of "service outsourcing"; Detailed division of labor (separation of technology, operations, and conversion). The core function is to drive the black market from "manual workshops" to "industrialized assembly lines." 4. High-Risk Transactions This type of business is the final application scenario for the aforementioned three types, directly corresponding to specific illegal and criminal activities. Main types include: Gambling-related: platform top-ups, points redemption, fund repatriation; Pornography and sugar daddies: transaction matching, long-term relationship maintenance, etc.; Human trafficking/illegal labor: cross-border labor transfer, intermediary matching; Data and account transactions: personal information, social media accounts, e-commerce accounts, etc. Core characteristics: Highly non-standardized transaction targets; Strong reliance on guarantee mechanisms to reduce fraud risk. The simultaneous existence of funding and resource needs. Its essential function is to utilize the aforementioned other types of services to ultimately complete the illegal business operations and fund laundering. From an overall perspective, the development of Telegram's escrow platform exhibits certain phased evolutionary characteristics, but this "phase division" is more based on observable structural changes than on strict organizational succession relationships. Furthermore, these evolutions are often accompanied by increased regulatory scrutiny. The following analysis examines the evolution and development process using leading escrow platforms from each stage as examples. Figure 2: Development Path of Mainstream Guarantee Platforms 1. Huione Guarantee Stands Out Huione is a relatively mature form of early Telegram guarantee market, whose core feature is establishing transaction order around "platform credit". During this phase, Huione Guarantee, through a relatively stable merchant system (mainly focused on financial businesses such as acceptance and card trading), established a fixed guarantee group, a deposit mechanism, and basic arbitration rules, shifting transaction behavior from decentralized matching to a platform-based organization. It once surpassed Hydra to become the largest underground black market trading market at the time. The structural characteristics of this phase are mainly manifested in: a credit constraint mechanism centered on platform rules and reputation; a steady increase in the number of merchants, far ahead of other platforms; and funds concentrated in a few core addresses, exhibiting strong aggregation characteristics. However, due to this highly concentrated structure, this model has relatively limited resilience against external regulatory shocks. Subsequently, under relevant crackdowns and restrictions, its ecosystem activity fluctuated significantly. 2. Tudou Guarantee leads the market After Huione's activity declined, market demand did not disappear, but shifted to other guarantee platforms. Tudou Guarantee took over most of Huione's market demand during this process, and its activity increased significantly. At this stage, Tudou Guarantee became the market leader of the new generation of guarantee services, but other platforms also gradually became active. This was mainly due to the huge market demand dispersed by Huione and the strong centralized risks exposed, which led to market differentiation. However, because Huione held a portion of Tudou Guarantee's shares, most of its merchants migrated to Tudou to continue their business, causing its market share and transaction volume to surge rapidly in a short period of time, showing a trend of quickly catching up with Huione, and even becoming the third largest underground black industry trading market after Huione and the dark web. However, this situation did not last. Affected by the US Treasury Department's sanctions against the Prince Group of Cambodia, after the Prince Group's founder was arrested, Tudou Guarantee had to declare its collapse under enormous political pressure. 3. Xinbi Guarantee Emerges as a Leader From Huione to Tudou, it's clear that market demand doesn't disappear. Even if leading platforms face enforcement or regulatory pressure and shut down, creating a temporary market vacuum, a new platform will inevitably emerge to fill that gap. Xinbi Guarantee is precisely the next player to fill this demand after Tudou's collapse. Although during this period, influenced by the regulatory enforcement actions of Huione and Tudou, some merchants began to diversify their risks and reduce their reliance on a single market, turning to other influential guarantee service markets, Xinbi, with its innovative content, gradually developed into a leader in the guarantee market during this phase. The platform standardizes the transaction process by introducing bots, including automated order creation, fund custody, token release, and partial arbitration functions. It also builds a more complete merchant management and traffic distribution system, gradually forming a multi-layered ecosystem of "guarantee + merchant + agent + traffic." This marks Telegram's entry into a systematic and automated escrow process, where its core is no longer just about matching transactions, but about improving overall operational efficiency and anonymity through technological means. The main characteristics of this stage include: The automation level has been significantly improved (reducing reliance on manual labor) The operation of multiple addresses and multiple roles (reducing single point of failure risk) The platform is closer to a "trading operating system" than a single market However, as the scale expands, its risk exposure also increases. On March 26, 2026, Xinbi Guarantee's parent company, Xinbi Group, was sanctioned by the UK. This directly led to a significant contraction of its related ecosystem recently, and some Telegram guarantee businesses were forced to suspend or reduce their activity. This event has once again had a significant impact on the entire Telegram escrow system: 1) Short-term impact: Some leading merchants have suspended transactions or entered a wait-and-see mode; capital turnover efficiency has decreased; market confidence has been impacted. 2) Structural changes: Business migration and the emergence of alternative tools. Against this backdrop, some practitioners (including Xinbi Escrow) have begun to promote the migration of their businesses to new tools, with the promotion and use of SafeW software being a prime example. These tools attempt to:

  • decouple from dependence on the single Telegram platform

  • provide a more closed or private trading environment

  • continue the escrow function while improving anti-ban capabilities

Figure 3: Xinbi Escrow Platform Migration Announcement

1. Overall Fund Size Change Characteristics: Event-Driven Non-linear Growth Structure

We analyzed the monthly changes in the total funds of these deposit addresses: From the perspective of fund inflows over the past year, the fund size of the guarantee platform exhibits obvious non-linear change characteristics, not following a stable growth or cyclical fluctuation pattern, but rather showing a typical event-driven leap structure.

Figure 4: Changes in the total amount of pledged funds on existing guarantee platforms in the past year. (1) Characteristics of phased changes. April-October 2025: Low-level stable phase (tens of millions) The market is currently in a state of fragmented competition across multiple platforms. Individual platforms have relatively small capital volumes, and no concentration effect has been achieved. Huione dominates the market, while existing platforms have low market share. November 2025: First leap (approximately 177M USD) Capital size increased more than 20 times compared to the previous period. December 2025 – January 2026: Continued high levels (2.4B – 2.5B USD) Market enters a highly concentrated phase February 2026 – March 2026: High-level fluctuations (1.6B – 2.1B USD) Funds begin to diverge, but remain at high levels April 2026: Slight decline (approximately 92M USD) (2) Analysis of core driving factors The two leaps in capital scale were mainly driven by two key events: ① November 2025: Huione Group was sanctioned Huione Group, the parent company of Huione Guarantee, was targeted by the international law enforcement and sanctions system, which directly led to the failure of the original core liquidity center of the guarantee market. Although most of the funds entered Tudou Guarantee, it can be seen that a large amount of funds still migrated to other guarantee platforms, which is essentially the first structural capital redistribution.

② January 2026: Tudou Guarantee Shuts Down

Tudou Guarantee was forced to shut down due to the investigation related to the "Prince Group case," leading to a second contraction in the supply side of leading companies. The impact was manifested in the following ways:

  • Short-term stagnation of high-level funds

  • Subsequent concentration on remaining leading platforms

  • Further decline in market trust

Essentially, it was a second structural concentration reinforcement + confidence decline

It is worth noting that in April of this year (as of the statistical date of April 22), the total amount of funds of guarantee platforms was estimated at approximately 1.22 billion USD based on the full month's data, a decrease of approximately 40% compared to the previous month.

It is worth noting that in April of this year (as of the statistical date of April 22), the total amount of funds of guarantee platforms was estimated at approximately 1.22 billion USD, a decrease of approximately 40% compared to the previous month.

Figure 6: Xinbi guarantee platform address risk situation

V. Future Development Trend Prediction

The sanctions imposed on Huione, Tudou, and Xinbi Escrow are not isolated incidents, but rather a phased result of the continuous regulatory impact on the underground trading system. However, unlike in the past, Xinbi Escrow's adjustment path in responding to sanctions may mark a new evolutionary stage for the Telegram escrow ecosystem.

The sanctions imposed on Huione, Tudou, and Xinbi Escrow are not isolated incidents, but rather a phased result of the continuous regulatory impact on the underground trading system. However, unlike in the past, Xinbi Escrow's adjustment path in responding to sanctions may mark a new evolutionary stage for the Telegram escrow ecosystem.

Figure 8: Fund Flow Path of Xinbi Guarantee

Furthermore, the emergence of tools such as SafeW has gradually brought Telegram's guarantee service into a "tool-based stage." During this phase, guarantee capabilities are standardized and the barriers to entry are lowered, allowing any small team with sufficient resources to quickly build similar functional modules, thereby weakening the control of leading platforms. Simultaneously, the trading environment becomes more closed, no longer entirely dependent on the public Telegram ecosystem. This change means that the identification target, originally centered on the "platform," is shifting to a large number of dispersed and dynamically changing nodes, with on-chain behavior exhibiting stronger fragmentation characteristics. Overall, the Telegram guarantee ecosystem is evolving from a "centralized market dominated by leading platforms" to a "technology-driven distributed network." In this process, risk dispersion, structural concealment, and replicable capabilities become key characteristics, posing a continuous challenge to traditional analytical methods that rely on static identification and centralized targets. In summary, the evolution of the Telegram escrow ecosystem is essentially a continuous reconstruction of the "credit and financial infrastructure" within the underground economy. Under sustained regulatory pressure, its operational model has gradually shifted from an early centralized market reliant on leading platforms to a networked structure characterized by technology-driven, decentralized nodes. This transformation has not weakened its overall function; rather, it has enhanced the system's survivability and replicability through decentralization and tool-based approaches, making it more difficult to effectively curb through single-point crackdowns. In this context, regulatory and AML practices also need to adjust their approaches accordingly. For regulators, simply sanctioning platforms is becoming increasingly ineffective. A more effective approach in the future lies in shifting from "targeting a single entity" to "constraining critical infrastructure." This includes strengthening cross-border collaboration, linking exchanges and stablecoin issuers to restrict the flow of funds, and implementing entry-level governance for key processes such as acceptance and money laundering to compress the space for fund transfers at the source. Simultaneously, attention to the proliferation of new tools and technologies will become more crucial, and the scope of regulation needs to gradually extend from the application layer to the technology and service layers supporting their operation. For AML vendors, traditional identification methods relying on address tags and static rules will struggle to adapt to highly dynamic and fragmented financial networks. Future efforts should focus on strengthening dynamic clustering and cross-cycle correlation capabilities, shifting from "identifying addresses" to "identifying networks," and combining off-chain intelligence (such as Telegram groups, bots, and merchant information) to achieve on-chain and off-chain联动 analysis. Furthermore, introducing higher-frequency monitoring and early warning mechanisms to improve response speed to emerging nodes and structural changes will also become a key competitive advantage. Currently, some solutions in the market have begun to develop in this direction. For example, platforms such as SkyInsights[4] are trying to integrate on-chain analysis and off-chain intelligence capabilities to support more dynamic risk identification and investigation.

Overall, the changes in Telegram's escrow system are driving regulation and anti-money laundering from "static identification of centralized targets" to "dynamic identification of distributed networks." Whoever can complete this paradigm shift first is more likely to take the initiative in the new stage of regulatory confrontation.
